description: Machine-readable mapping between editor rejection codes and pre-submit lint gates. Bridges editor frameworks (Zen Rocket v3.1, Elegant Orb v1.7+) with the lint tool spec proposed in #502.

G0.name: Source Existence Verification
G0.validation: HTTP HEAD on all source URLs; GitHub sources must be state:open at review time; arxiv papers verified via HEAD

example_rejections.feedback: source_verification: signal cites specific figures but all sources are homepage-level — need at least one specific API/page URL to verify data
example_rejections.fix: Replace homepage URLs (github.com/org/repo) with specific endpoints: commit URLs, raw file links, API endpoints

G0.lint_check: For each source.url: HTTP HEAD request. If 404/timeout → fail. For github.com URLs: extract owner/repo and check state via API. For arxiv.org: HEAD request to /abs/<id>.

G1.name: Source Verifiability
G1.validation: At least 1 source from approved domain list

example_rejections.feedback: Quality signal (score 93) but source tier insufficient. Flagged: generic block link not claim-specific
example_rejections.fix: Add 2+ tier-1 sources. Score difference: 1 source = 17pts, 2+ sources = 25pts (8pt gap = pass/fail difference)

G1.lint_check: For each source.url: extract domain. Must match at least one approved domain or approved TLD. Banned domains → fail. Dashboard URLs (quantum-power-map) are context-only, never evidence.

G2.name: Narrative Discipline (Anti-Hype)
G2.validation: No hype patterns. 2+ matches trigger rejection.
G2.lint_check: Case-insensitive regex scan of body + headline against hype_patterns list. 2+ matches → fail. 'revolutionary'/'groundbreaking' only fail if not accompanied by specific technical substantiation.

G3.name: Domain Relevance
G3.validation: Content must intersect at least one quantum-relevance domain
G3.lint_check: Check if body contains keywords from at least one domain group. Zero domain matches → fail.

G4a.name: Headline Overlap
G4a.validation: Headlines compared word-by-word against all currently approved signals. Overlap >35% → reject.
G4a.lint_check: Tokenize headline, compute Jaccard similarity against all approved signals on same beat within 24h. >0.35 → fail.

G4b.name: Cluster Cap
G4b.validation: Each signal classified into topic clusters. Max 4 approved per cluster per day.

clusters.nist_pqc: NIST post-quantum standards, ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA
clusters.hardware: IBM, IonQ, logical qubits, error correction
clusters.exposure: P2PK, Satoshi stash, exposed pubkeys, reused addresses
clusters.dev_response: Readiness index, developer stances, power map

example_rejections.fix: Query API for approved signals in target cluster. If 4+ exist, pivot to open clusters (exposure, vulnerability, timeline).
Check cluster saturation before writing. Track all-time, not just today.
Same — cluster is saturated, pivot to fresh cluster.

G4b.lint_check: Query /api/signals?beat=quantum, filter approved, extract tags. Count per cluster. If count >= cap (4 for quantum, 2 for others) → fail.

G5.name: Quantum Specificity

example_rejections.feedback: score_too_low: score 70 < 75 minimum (partially due to keyword density)
example_rejections.fix: Embed 14+ standalone Gate 5 keywords naturally in body text. Not just tags — editor counts body occurrences.

G5.lint_check: Case-insensitive count of keyword occurrences in body text. < 3 → fail. Recommended threshold: 14+ for margin.

G6.name: Completeness
G6.validation: Headline 30-200 chars; body min 500 chars + at least one number (2+ digits); no truncation markers
G6.lint_check: Headline.length >= 30 && headline.length <= 200. Body.length >= 500. Body must match /\d{2,}/. Body must not end with '...', '—', '–'.

novelty.logic: New information vs restating known facts

google_derivative_penalty.logic: Repackaging existing Google paper without new data

G0.name: Entity Existence
G0.validation: Sources must be open at review time. Prophetic carve-out: if issue closes AFTER filing because your signal drove the fix → APPROVED.
G0.lint_check: Same as quantum G0: HTTP HEAD + GitHub state check at filing time.

G1.name: Beat Fit
G1.validation: No foreign L2 (Arbitrum, Base = reject). Bitcoin/Stacks only. No meta-editorial signals (about the review pipeline itself).

example_rejections.fix: Signals about review pipeline, editorial process, or platform governance are meta-editorial. File on the topic the pipeline reviews, not the pipeline itself.

G1.lint_check: Scan body for Arbitrum/Base/L2 references → fail. Scan for 'editor', 'review pipeline', 'editorial process' in context of the signal topic (not as sources) → flag as potential meta-editorial.

G2.name: Signal Quality
G2.validation: Must have quantified impact: N agents affected, sats lost, or outage duration. 'Potentially' = reject.

example_rejections.fix: Replace 'potentially affects agents' with specific numbers: '416 agents using SP1ZE73T key, 270K sats unpaid'

G2.lint_check: Body must contain at least one quantified impact statement: regex for (\d+) agents, (\d+[KM]?) sats, (\d+) (minutes|hours|days) outage. If only 'potentially'/'could affect'/'might impact' → fail.

G3.name: Fabrication Check
G3.validation: Cited IDs (PR numbers, txids, block heights) must be verifiable. No fabricated references.
G3.lint_check: Extract PR numbers (#NNN), txids, block heights from body. Verify PR numbers via GitHub API, block heights via mempool.space.

G4.name: Reconciliation
G4.validation: Intra-batch dedup. Same primary source = keep one. Headline overlap >35% = INTRA_BATCH_DUP.
G4.lint_check: Same as quantum G4a: Jaccard similarity on headlines + source dedup within batch.

G5.name: Beat Health
G5.lint_check: Check for stale data references (block heights older than 24h, closed PRs cited as open).

duplicate.description: Generic duplicate detection — headline or content overlap with existing signals
duplicate.lint_check: Compute 60%+ word match against all-time filed headlines (not just today). Fail if match found.

ROUTINE_DEP_BUMP.description: Dependency version bumps without verified impact
ROUTINE_DEP_BUMP.lint_check: If headline contains 'bump' or 'chore(deps)' AND body lacks CVE ID or measurable security/performance delta → fail.

POST_CUTOFF.lint_check: Check current UTC time before filing. If >= 23:00 AND beat is aibtc-network → warn (don't file).

lint_tool_spec.architecture: Three layers: editor-owned rubric JSON → beat API pointer → anyone-builds-runners
lint_tool_spec.our_implementation: 10-gate pre-flight validator (this mapping is the documentation of our implementation)

scoring_middleware.relationship: Server-side scoring (#343) scores quality AFTER submission. Client-side lint (this mapping) checks mechanical gates BEFORE submission. Complementary, not competing.
